# SRC021 - Diffusion of Innovations Theory Review

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Software Adoption |
| Authors / source | Sahin / Rogers |
| Year | 2006 / 2003 |
| Source type | Academic review of classic theory |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/ED501453.pdf](https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/ED501453.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Educational review; theory-level.

## Core takeaway

Adoption accelerates with relative advantage, compatibility, simplicity, trialability, and observability.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to design trial, demo, proof, and compatibility messages.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC021
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
